name: AMBIENT
desc: Plays by itself, meant to evoke or engage intermittent attention. Such work does not require or particularly invite a focused reading session. See also: LOCATIVE.

name: ANIMATED
desc: Moving images and/or text, which may be accompanied by sound

name: APPROPRIATED
desc: Incorporates elements not composed by the authors, but collected or mined from online or print media, using content from more than one source in a new configuration.

name: AUDIO COLLAGE
desc: Repurposes previously recorded audio (unrelated to the work’s composition) to have an additional or new impact for purposes of estrangement.

name: AUGMENTED REALITY
desc: The augmenting or overlaying of a real, physical environment with virtual, computer-generated graphics and sometimes sound in real time.

name: CODEWORK
desc: Creative writing which in some way references or incorporates formal computer languages within the text. The code may not necessarily compile or run, though compilable code may be included as a form of constraint.

name: COLLABORATIVE
desc: Cohesive whole resulting from individual contributions and teamwork, often across disciplines or specialties.

name: COMBINATORIAL
desc: Distinct media segments are algorithmically arranged and re-arranged to create multiplicative outputs.

name: CONCEPTUAL
desc: Concepts or ideas are considered the real substance of the work, as opposed to the artifact.

name: DATABASE
desc: Composed of, structured by, or otherwise incorporating an organized collection of data, either technically or metaphorically.

name: GENERATIVE
desc: Incorporates processing in the production of recombinatory outputs.

name: HACKTIVIST
desc: Procedurality intervenes in or subverts the intended purpose of software or platforms.

name: HAPTIC
desc: Gesture-based and/or tactile interaction to produce embodied involvement. Distinct from simple navigation or selection.

name: HYPERTEXT
desc: Segments (also called “lexia”) connected by and navigated through linked words, phrases or images.

name: INSTALLATION
desc: Fixed or site specific, built in a physical environment; uses digital processing and is often engaged or viewed by multiple participants.

name: INTERACTIVE FICTION
desc: Primarily engaged through text entry and system responses which incorporate simulated environments and/or virtual agents. Often abbreviated “IF” and distinguished from hyperfiction as “parser-based.”

name: KINETIC
desc: Emphasis on simulated physical motion, as in kinetic typography.

name: LITERARY GAME
desc: Incorporates gamelike elements such as win-states, points, rules or ironic deployment of game features for critical commentary.

name: LOCATIVE
desc: Delivered through the location technologies of smartphones and tablets to enable a situated, medially layered reading experience that responds to its environs.

name: MASH-UP
desc: Combines multiple appropriated texts or softwares to make a new work.

name: NANOGENMO
desc: National Novel Generation Month’s goal is to write code that generates novels. NaNoGenMo is based on community engagement, where participants share at least one generated novel, and distribute the source code. See also: GENERATIVE.

name: NETWORKED
desc: Structures that mimic or make use of existing modes of discourse common to the web, such as blogs, websites and social media.

name: PARTICIPATORY
desc: Distinct from COLLABORATIVE, invites and incorporates contributions without altering the fundamental authorial design of the work.

name: PERFORMANCE
desc: Designed to be an ephemeral and unrepeatable experience enacted by human or nonhuman agents; can be preserved only through documentation.

name: PROCEDURAL
desc: A rule-based system that generates meaningful output.

name: PROCEDURAL RHETORIC
desc: Enforces actions through rules to comment on structures or systems in the broader world.

name: RETRO
desc: Evokes obsolete technology and media, imitating or incorporating no longer mainstream interfaces and aesthetics.

name: SERIOUS GAME
desc: Imparts meaning that exceeds entertainment, and provokes thought or commentary on a topic of controversy or concern.

name: TEXT MOVIE
desc: A long flow of self-performing text, which may be "choreographed" to music or driven by an algorithm.

name: VIRTUAL ENVIRONMENT
desc: An immersive, simulated experience typically engaged through a head-mounted display.

name: VISUAL POETRY
desc: Dissolving boundaries between fine arts and literature, places verbal and typographic elements in dynamic interaction with iconography.

name: WORDTOY
desc: Invites the user to manipulate text, more playful than for reading comprehension.
